---
title: Package Selection
description: Choose the right Elata package and avoid internal-only workflows.
---

## Recommended Default

If you are starting fresh, use `@elata-biosciences/create-elata-demo`.

That is the shortest path to a running app and the best starting point for
evaluation, onboarding, and comparison debugging.

## What These Packages Help You Build

- **EEG packages** are for apps that use brain-signal data from a headset.
  Typical use cases include meditation or focus UX, neurofeedback, adaptive
  gameplay, dashboards, and simulated athletic-performance or training
  experiences that react to live headset input or EEG-derived features.
- **rPPG packages** are for apps that use a regular camera to estimate
  pulse-related signals. Typical use cases include wellness flows, readiness
  checks, deception or bluffing mechanics, biofeedback-style experiences, and
  camera-based vitals prototypes.

## Choose By Outcome

| You want to... | Use this | Notes |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Scaffold a new demo app | `@elata-biosciences/create-elata-demo` | recommended first step |
| Run EEG WASM APIs in the browser | `@elata-biosciences/eeg-web` | signal processing, models, and WASM helpers |
| Connect to Muse-compatible devices in the browser | `@elata-biosciences/eeg-web-ble` | install alongside `eeg-web` |
| Run camera-based rPPG in a browser app | `@elata-biosciences/rppg-web` | start with `createRppgSession()` |

## Browser EEG

Use `@elata-biosciences/eeg-web` when you need:

- browser-side EEG WASM APIs
- signal processing and model functions
- shared types used by higher-level browser integrations

In app terms, this is the package you use when your product logic needs EEG
features or analyses in the browser for things like meditation state, focus
feedback, neurofeedback, or performance-style training loops.

## Browser BLE For Muse-Compatible Devices

Use `@elata-biosciences/eeg-web-ble` when you need:

- browser BLE discovery and streaming
- normalized headband frames
- a transport layer that plugs into the Elata EEG web stack

You should generally install `@elata-biosciences/eeg-web` alongside it.

In app terms, this is the package that gets real headset data into the browser.

## Camera-Based rPPG

Use `@elata-biosciences/rppg-web` when you need:

- browser-side rPPG processing
- packaged WASM backend loading
- demo helpers for camera-driven prototypes

In app terms, this is the package you use when the camera itself is the sensor
and your app wants pulse-style metrics without extra hardware for things like
stress-aware UX, deception mechanics, or biofeedback-oriented health flows.

## Internal Paths To Avoid Unless You Mean It

- Do not start new apps with `./run.sh sync-to`.
- Do not treat in-repo demos as the primary consumer onboarding path.
- Do not assume a scaffolded app is broken until you check whether `pnpm`
  attached to a parent workspace.

## When The Alternative Path Is Correct

Only use `./run.sh sync-to` when you are actively modifying
`packages/eeg-web` inside this monorepo and want to install the local package
into another app for iteration.
